hypothesis: owner tails encode state/layout payload rather than target ordinals
note: Companion tails already carry backlink-like ordinals; owner tails differ in both length and byte family.

hypothesis: a40902 is a compact owner-state triplet tied to enabled/active mode
note: Sits in owner Up slot, short fixed form, plausibly boolean/activation style.

hypothesis: a4072dc41001 is an owner-layout tuple with embedded scalar 0x10
note: Longer form unique to Right slot; likely packs layout/render metadata not present in companion.

hypothesis: 0x2DC4 inside Right owner tail is a packed local layout/ref value
note: Looks ref-like but not yet mapped to object ordinals.

hypothesis: Down owner/companion tails form a shared-list root control pair
note: Shared ordinal 0x00F5 and root-level evidence from Stage 93 support this.
